title: [HW] Extend ElementType parsing to support union types
body:
This PR fixes a bug where `!hw.union` types nested inside `!hw.array` failed to parse back in after being printed (round-trip failure).

```python
from circt.ir import Context, Location, Type
import circt

with Context() as ctx, Location.unknown():
    circt.register_dialects(ctx)
    t = Type.parse("!hw.array<4x!hw.union<u8: i8, u16: i16>>")
    print(t)  # !hw.array<4xunion<u8: i8, u16: i16>>
    Type.parse(str(t))   # this fails
    # MLIRError: Unable to parse type:
    #error: "!hw.array<4xunion<u8: i8, u16: i16>>":1:13: expected non-function type
```

When printing nested types, `printHWElementType` elides the dialect prefix to produce a more readable output (e.g., `union<...>` instead of `!hw.union<...>`).

However, the corresponding parser helper, `parseHWElementType`, relies on a hardcoded list of string prefixes to detect when a bare keyword is being used. This list currently includes `struct`, `array`, `enum`, etc., but is missing `union`.

As a result, the parser fails to recognize `union<...>` as a valid HW type when parsing the output of a valid IR print.
